rxn_class_summary: Pulse radiolysis and spectroelectrochemical study of bipyridine-ligated Ni(I) bromide intermediates relevant to Ni-catalyzed cross-coupling, characterizing Ni(0)/Ni(II) comproportionation and Ni(I) oxidative addition with aryl iodides.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic two-electron reduction of (dtbbpy)NiBr2 generates Ni(0), which rapidly comproportionates with remaining Ni(II) to give the catalytically relevant Ni(I) monomer; pulse radiolysis alternatively delivers a single solvated electron to (dtbbpy)NiBr2 to directly generate [(dtbbpy)NiBr].
electron_flow: A solvated electron (from pulse radiolysis) or cathode reduces (dtbbpy)NiBr2; under bulk electrolysis a 2e- reduction yields Ni(0) which comproportionates with Ni(II) to give two Ni(I)Br monomers, which then undergo rapid oxidative addition with aryl iodides to give Ni(III)(Ar)(Br)(I).
rate_determining_step: Oxidative addition of aryl iodide to [(dtbbpy)NiBr] (ΔG‡298K = 10.4 ± 0.4 kcal/mol; ΔH‡ = 5.8 kcal/mol, ΔS‡ = -15.5 cal/mol/K)

probe_reagents.name: 4-bromobenzotrifluoride
probe_reagents.observation: No significant decay of Ni(I) signal; upper limit kOA < 10^4 M-1 s-1 for aryl bromide OA

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 4-iodobenzoate
probe_reagents.observation: Significant Ni(I) signal quenching, kOA = 1.18 × 10^5 M-1 s-1

probe_reagents.name: 1,3-dicyanobenzene
probe_reagents.observation: Used as electron-transfer mediator to ensure efficient Ni(I) generation at high aryl iodide concentrations

probe_reagents.name: iodobenzene (vs synthesized [(dtbbpy)NiBr]2 dimer)
probe_reagents.observation: Monomer reacts rapidly (kOA = 2.2 × 10^4 M-1 s-1); pre-formed dimer shows no reaction with PhI over 16 h by 1H NMR

probe_reagents.name: panel of 4-substituted aryl iodides (OMe, H, OCF3, CO2Et, CN)
probe_reagents.observation: Hammett ρ = +3.1 (R2 = 0.91); 18-fold rate increase across σ = -0.27 to +0.66, consistent with electron-rich Ni(I) attacking electrophilic arene

notes: Two competing pathways for Ni(I) generation are distinguished: (i) direct 1e- reduction (pulse radiolysis) vs (ii) 2e- cathodic reduction followed by Ni(0)/Ni(II) comproportionation (bulk electrolysis) — both converge on the same [(dtbbpy)NiBr] monomer (matching UV-vis at 430, 620, 860 nm). Dimerization to [(dtbbpy)NiBr]2 occurs on the minute timescale and shunts catalyst into an unreactive off-cycle resting state, with implications for cross-coupling reactions run at high Ni concentrations. Eyring analysis provides experimental benchmark for DFT studies of Ni(I) OA barriers in Ni/photoredox catalysis. The OA could in principle proceed via Ni(I)/Ni(III) (favored here) or Ni(0)/Ni(II) (alternative complicated by comproportionation).
